_metadata.title: QIF Receptor and Ion Channel Database
_metadata.description: 20 receptor/channel families with gene names, subunit composition, signaling mechanisms, pharmacological agents, and BCI/security relevance. For threat modeling purposes — not clinical diagnosis.
notes: Gene names follow HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee (HGNC) conventions.
NCBI Gene IDs verified against NCBI Gene database.
DSM mappings are diagnostic category references for threat modeling purposes, not diagnostic claims.
BCI relevance reflects signal-level and pharmacological interactions, not cognitive claims.
Confidence: HIGH = multiple verified sources, MEDIUM = single verified source, LOW = inferred.

receptor_families.name: NMDA Receptors

subunits.name: GluN1
subunits.notes: Obligate subunit; glycine/D-serine co-agonist binding site

subunits.name: GluN2B
subunits.expression: High embryonic; maintained in adult hippocampus

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Ligand-gated + voltage-dependent (coincidence detector). High Ca2+ permeability drives synaptic plasticity (LTP/LTD).
dependency_chain: GlyT1 (SLC6A9) and GlyT2 (SLC6A5) regulate glycine availability at co-agonist site
D-serine synthesized by serine racemase (SRR); degraded by D-amino acid oxidase (DAO)
Ca2+ influx activates CaMKII, triggering LTP
receptor_families.function: Coincidence detector for synaptic plasticity; critical for LTP, learning, memory formation, excitatory neurotransmission

dsm_conditions.name: GRIN Disorder Encephalopathies
dsm_conditions.mechanism: GRIN1/2A/2B loss-of-function: DEE, intellectual disability, epilepsy

receptor_families.bci_relevance: High. NMDA receptors gate synaptic plasticity underlying motor learning encoded by BCI feedback loops. Ketamine alters EEG power spectral density (increased gamma, decreased delta) in patterns measurable by BCI. Adversarial neurostimulation targeting coincidence-detection windows could theoretically disrupt LTP encoding.

receptor_families.name: AMPA Receptors

subunits.name: GluA1
subunits.notes: Both flip/flop variants; flip desensitizes slower

subunits.name: GluA2
subunits.notes: Critical R/G editing site (unedited = Ca2+ permeable)

receptor_families.ion_selectivity: Na+, K+ (Ca2+ permeable if GluA2 unedited)
receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Ionotropic; tetrameric. Ca2+ permeability determined by GluA2 RNA editing at Q/R site (codon 607). Edited GluA2 (R form, >99% adult brain) renders receptor Ca2+-impermeable. Flip variants desensitize slower than flop variants.
dependency_chain: RNA editing by ADAR2 (ADARB1) at GluA2 Q607R site — near-universal in adult brain
TARPs (e.g., stargazin/CACNG2) required for membrane trafficking and gating
PICK1, GRIP proteins regulate surface expression and recycling
receptor_families.function: Fast excitatory synaptic transmission; primary mediator of depolarization at excitatory synapses; AMPA receptor insertion drives LTP expression
receptor_families.bci_relevance: Moderate-High. AMPA receptors mediate the fast depolarization component of ERPs measurable in EEG. AMPAkines have been explored to enhance BCI signal-to-noise during motor imagery tasks. Flip/flop ratio affects temporal resolution of neural coding.

receptor_families.name: Kainate Receptors
receptor_families.ion_selectivity: Na+, K+, Ca2+ (GluK1-3 homomers Ca2+ permeable if unedited)
receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Dual role: ionotropic (fast excitation, Na+/K+/Ca2+ permeable) and metabotropic-like (presynaptic modulation via Gq/Gi signaling, PTX-sensitive G-protein). Presynaptically reduce GABA release in hippocampus (pro-epileptic).
brain_regions: hippocampus (GluK2/GluK5 widespread; GluK3 dentate gyrus; GluK4 CA3)
receptor_families.function: Presynaptic modulation of glutamate and GABA release; mossy fiber LTP in CA3; regulation of network excitability

pharmacological_agents.name: NS1209
pharmacological_agents.notes: Phase II trial for status epilepticus

pharmacological_agents.name: Kainate
pharmacological_agents.notes: Tool compound; neurotoxic at high doses

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Low-moderate. Kainate receptor activation alters hippocampal theta oscillations detectable in intracranial EEG; relevant to memory BCI applications.

receptor_families.name: Metabotropic Glutamate Receptors

subunits.name: mGluR5
subunits.expression: Hippocampus, amygdala, striatum, NAc, dorsal horn

subunits.name: mGluR3
subunits.expression: Pre- and postsynaptic; glia; cortex, hippocampus

subunits.name: mGluR8
subunits.expression: Presynaptic; olfactory bulb, lateral nucleus amygdala

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Group I (GRM1, GRM5): Gq/11 → PLC → IP3 + DAG → Ca2+ release from ER + PKC activation. Postsynaptic; amplify NMDA responses. Groups II/III (GRM2-4, GRM6-8): Gi/o → inhibit adenylyl cyclase → reduced cAMP. Predominantly presynaptic autoreceptors; reduce glutamate release.
receptor_families.function: Group I: postsynaptic amplification of excitatory signaling, synaptic plasticity. Groups II/III: presynaptic autoreceptors reducing glutamate release.
receptor_families.bci_relevance: Moderate. mGluR5 modulates synaptic plasticity at theta-burst intervals used in TMS-BCI protocols. mGluR2/3 agonism reduces signal-to-noise in glutamatergic readout.

receptor_families.name: GABA-A Receptors

subunits.name: alpha4
subunits.notes: Dentate gyrus, thalamus; BZD-insensitive; tonic inhibition

subunits.name: gamma2
subunits.notes: Most abundant; required for synaptic targeting

receptor_families.ion_selectivity: Cl- (hyperpolarizing in adults; depolarizing in early development due to KCC2/NKCC1 ratio)
receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Pentameric Cl- channel. Cl- influx → hyperpolarization → reduced firing probability. Multiple allosteric sites: BZD site (alpha+/gamma- interface), barbiturate site (beta TM domain), neurosteroid site (intramembrane alpha/beta), ethanol site (TM2-3 linker), picrotoxin site (channel pore).

allosteric_sites.name: Benzodiazepine site
allosteric_sites.mechanism: PAM; increases Cl- channel open frequency

allosteric_sites.name: Barbiturate site
allosteric_sites.mechanism: Increases channel open duration; at high doses, directly activates

dependency_chain: KCC2 (SLC12A5) extrusion of Cl- maintains hyperpolarizing gradient in adults; loss causes excitatory GABA
NKCC1 (SLC12A2) imports Cl- in immature neurons; targeted by bumetanide
Gephyrin scaffold protein required for synaptic clustering
GABARAP proteins required for trafficking
receptor_families.function: Primary fast inhibitory neurotransmission; sets E/I balance; multiple allosteric modulation sites for pharmacological intervention
receptor_families.bci_relevance: High. GABA-A tone sets the E/I balance measurable as EEG oscillatory power (beta, alpha bands). Benzodiazepines profoundly alter EEG signatures used by BCI decoders. Neurosteroid modulation affects sleep-stage EEG patterns used in closed-loop neurostimulation. GABA-A is primary target of most anesthetic agents that confound intraoperative neural recording.

receptor_families.name: GABA-B Receptors

subunits.name: GABA-B1
subunits.notes: Isoforms 1a (presynaptic, sushi domains, APP-interacting) and 1b (postsynaptic, dendritic). Chromosome 6p21.3 (HLA region)

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Gi/o coupling → inhibition of adenylyl cyclase + activation of GIRK (Kir3) K+ channels (postsynaptic hyperpolarization) + inhibition of voltage-gated Ca2+ channels (Cav2.1, Cav2.2; reduces neurotransmitter release presynaptically).
receptor_families.function: Slow inhibitory neurotransmission; presynaptic auto- and heteroreceptor modulation; postsynaptic hyperpolarization via GIRK channels

pharmacological_agents.name: Baclofen
pharmacological_agents.notes: FDA-approved muscle relaxant; alcohol use disorder adjunct

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Moderate. Presynaptic GABA-B autoreceptors and heteroreceptors set gain on glutamatergic and GABAergic transmission; relevant to BCI calibration stability over long sessions.

receptor_families.name: Nicotinic Acetylcholine Receptors

key_brain_subtypes.name: alpha4-beta2
key_brain_subtypes.notes: Most abundant CNS nAChR; high-affinity nicotine binding site; cortex, thalamus, striatum

key_brain_subtypes.name: alpha7
key_brain_subtypes.notes: High Ca2+ permeability (~NMDA); rapid desensitization; hippocampus, cortex, thalamus; cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Pentameric Cys-loop receptor. Ligand binding opens cation channel → depolarization. alpha7: high Ca2+ conductance (comparable to NMDA) and fast desensitization. alpha4-beta2: slow, sustained excitation; slower desensitization.
receptor_families.function: Fast excitatory cholinergic neurotransmission; modulation of attention, arousal, and cognitive function; presynaptic facilitation of neurotransmitter release

dsm_conditions.name: Schizophrenia
dsm_conditions.mechanism: alpha7 CHRNA7 on chr 15q14 — schizophrenia susceptibility locus; reduced alpha7 in postmortem brains

receptor_families.bci_relevance: High. alpha7 nAChR modulates hippocampal theta/gamma coupling measurable in EEG; relevant to memory and attention BCIs. Nicotinic tone alters arousal-related EEG signatures (alpha suppression, gamma enhancement) used in closed-loop neurofeedback. Cholinergic depletion in Alzheimer's is a primary target for BCI-augmented cognitive neuroprosthetics.

receptor_families.name: Muscarinic Acetylcholine Receptors

subunits.name: M1
subunits.expression: Cortex (60% of total mAChR), hippocampus, striatum

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: M1, M3, M5 (odd-numbered): Gq/11 → PLC → IP3 + DAG → Ca2+ mobilization + PKC. M2, M4 (even-numbered): Gi/o → inhibit adenylyl cyclase + activate GIRK channels → hyperpolarization.
dependency_chain: Acetylcholine synthesized by ChAT (CHAT gene)
Degraded by AChE (ACHE) and BuChE (BCHE)
CHRM1/CHRM3 mRNA significantly reduced in Alzheimer's disease cortex/hippocampus
receptor_families.function: Modulation of cortical excitability, arousal, memory encoding; M1 gates cortical desynchronization; M2 presynaptic autoreceptor regulation

dsm_conditions.name: Alzheimer's Disease
dsm_conditions.mechanism: M1 loss — primary target; CHRM1/CHRM3 reduced

dsm_conditions.name: Schizophrenia
dsm_conditions.mechanism: M4 PAM as antipsychotic strategy; xanomeline+trospium FDA-approved 2024

receptor_families.bci_relevance: High. Muscarinic tone (particularly M1) gates cortical EEG desynchronization and arousal. Scopolamine produces EEG changes (increased theta, decreased alpha) mimicking early Alzheimer's — a confound for BCI systems relying on alpha-band motor imagery. M1 agonism is a primary target for cognitive neuroprosthetics.

receptor_families.name: Dopamine Receptors

subunits.name: D1
subunits.expression: Striatum, NAc, cortex, thalamus, amygdala, SNr

subunits.name: D2
subunits.expression: Striatum, NAc, VTA, cortex; presynaptic autoreceptor

subunits.name: D4
subunits.expression: Frontal cortex, hippocampus, retina, basal ganglia

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: D1-like (DRD1, DRD5): Gs → adenylyl cyclase activation → cAMP up → PKA → DARPP-32 phosphorylation, AMPA receptor trafficking, transcription (FosB, CREB). D2-like (DRD2, DRD3, DRD4): Gi/o → adenylyl cyclase inhibition → cAMP down + GIRK K+ channel activation + beta-arrestin 2 signaling (D2).
receptor_families.d2_splice_variants: D2-short (presynaptic autoreceptor function); D2-long (postsynaptic); differ by 29 amino acids in 3rd intracellular loop
dependency_chain: Dopamine synthesis: tyrosine → L-DOPA (TH gene) → dopamine (DDC gene)
Packaged by VMAT2 (SLC18A2)
Degraded by MAO-A/MAO-B (MAOA/MAOB), COMT
Reuptaken by DAT (SLC6A3)
receptor_families.function: Reward processing, motor control, motivation, working memory, reinforcement learning

dsm_conditions.name: Schizophrenia
dsm_conditions.mechanism: D2 hyperdopaminergia in mesolimbic; D1 hypodopaminergia in PFC

receptor_families.bci_relevance: High. Dopamine gates motor cortex excitability and reward-related learning. D2 receptor occupancy by antipsychotics reduces beta-band activity in motor cortex — major confound for motor BCI users. Dopaminergic state modulates P300 amplitude (BCI control signal) and sensorimotor mu rhythm. Closed-loop DBS of basal ganglia directly modulates dopaminergic circuits (DBS for Parkinson's).

receptor_families.name: Serotonin Receptors

subunits.name: 5-HT2C
subunits.expression: Choroid plexus, limbic system, hypothalamus, cortex

subunits.name: 5-HT3A
subunits.notes: Required for functional 5-HT3; can form homomers

receptor_families.ht3_note: The only ionotropic serotonin receptor. Pentameric Cys-loop structure. Functional receptors require 5-HT3A subunit. HTR3D and HTR3E are predominantly or exclusively GI tract.
dependency_chain: Serotonin synthesis: tryptophan → 5-HTP (TPH2 in brain; TPH1 in gut) → serotonin (DDC)
Reuptaken by SERT (SLC6A4)
Degraded by MAO-A
Tryptophan availability from diet is rate-limiting
receptor_families.function: Mood regulation, anxiety modulation, sleep-wake cycle, appetite, pain processing, cognitive function
receptor_families.bci_relevance: Moderate-High. 5-HT2A is the primary target of classic psychedelics (psilocybin, LSD), which produce profound broadband EEG changes (increased gamma entropy, decreased alpha power, disrupted DMN) that are now being mapped as altered BCI decoding states. SSRIs alter ERP components (P300, N200) used in BCI paradigms over weeks. 5-HT7 modulates hippocampal theta rhythm relevant to memory BCIs.

receptor_families.name: Adrenergic Receptors

subunits.name: beta2
subunits.g_protein: Gs (can switch to Gi via PKA)

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: alpha1 (ADRA1A/B/D): Gq/11 → PLC → IP3 + DAG → Ca2+ + PKC → postsynaptic excitation. alpha2 (ADRA2A/B/C): Gi/o → inhibit adenylyl cyclase → cAMP down + GIRK activation; primary presynaptic NE autoreceptor (ADRA2A dominant). beta1/2 (ADRB1/2): Gs → adenylyl cyclase → cAMP up → PKA; beta2 can switch to Gi via PKA phosphorylation (biased agonism).
dependency_chain: NE synthesis: tyrosine → L-DOPA (TH) → dopamine (DDC) → NE (DBH gene)
Packaged by VMAT2 (SLC18A2)
Reuptaken by NET (SLC6A2)
Degraded by MAO-A/MAO-B and COMT
receptor_families.function: Arousal, attention, stress response, working memory modulation, fight-or-flight

dsm_conditions.name: PTSD
dsm_conditions.mechanism: Prazosin alpha1 for nightmares; guanfacine alpha2A for hyperarousal

dsm_conditions.name: Anxiety Disorders
dsm_conditions.mechanism: Beta-blockers for performance anxiety; yohimbine challenge model

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Moderate. NE (via alpha2A) modulates prefrontal cortex working memory representations; guanfacine used in ADHD BCI studies to stabilize attention signals. Beta-adrenergic activation (arousal/stress) increases EEG noise (muscle artifact, altered alpha) that degrades BCI performance. Closed-loop DBS for pain modulates NE circuits.

receptor_families.name: Opioid Receptors

subunits.name: mu-opioid (MOR)
subunits.expression: PAG, thalamus, cortex, limbic, NAc, dorsal horn

subunits.name: delta-opioid (DOR)
subunits.expression: Cortex, NAc, amygdala, caudate, pontine nuclei

subunits.name: kappa-opioid (KOR)
subunits.expression: Hypothalamus, VTA, NAc, amygdala, dorsal horn

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: All four: Gi/o → inhibit adenylyl cyclase → cAMP down + activate GIRK K+ channels (hyperpolarization) + inhibit Cav2.2/Cav2.1 (reduce neurotransmitter release presynaptically). Beta-arrestin recruitment (especially OPRM1) mediates desensitization and receptor internalization.
dependency_chain: Beta-arrestin 2 (ARRB2) mediates OPRM1 internalization and tolerance development; GRK2/3 phosphorylation initiates
RGS proteins (RGS4, RGS9-2) accelerate GTPase activity, terminate Gi signaling
PDYN (prodynorphin), PENK (proenkephalin), POMC (proopiomelanocortin) are endogenous ligand precursors
Processed by PC1/PC2 (PCSK1/PCSK2) enzymes
receptor_families.function: Pain modulation, reward processing, stress response, respiratory regulation, mood

pharmacological_agents.name: Buprenorphine
pharmacological_agents.notes: Also KOR antagonist, DOR antagonist, NOP agonist

dsm_conditions.name: Opioid Use Disorder
dsm_conditions.mechanism: OPRM1 A118G polymorphism — altered endorphin binding

dsm_conditions.name: Major Depressive Disorder
dsm_conditions.mechanism: KOR antagonism — kappa tone mediates stress-induced dysphoria

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Moderate. Opioid receptor activation alters consciousness and EEG state globally (mu rhythms desynchronize with opioid administration; high-dose opioids produce burst-suppression). Opioid-dependent patients using neural prosthetics represent a risk population. KOR modulation of dopaminergic tone in VTA affects reward-learning signals used in BCI training paradigms.

receptor_families.name: Endocannabinoid Receptors

subunits.name: CB1
subunits.notes: Primary psychoactive target of THC; most abundant GPCR in brain

subunits.name: CB2
subunits.notes: Immune cells; astroglia in brain (limited neuronal expression under normal conditions)

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: CB1: Gi/o → inhibit adenylyl cyclase → cAMP down + inhibit Cav1/2 channels (reduce neurotransmitter release; primarily presynaptic retrograde signaling) + activate GIRK K+ channels. Operates via retrograde signaling: postsynaptic neurons synthesize 2-AG (via DAGL-alpha/DAGL-beta) or anandamide (via NAPE-PLD), which travel retrogradely to activate presynaptic CB1, suppressing further release (DSI/DSE).
dependency_chain: 2-AG synthesis: DAG → 2-AG (DAGL-alpha/DAGLB)
2-AG degradation: MAGL (primary), ABHD6, ABHD12
AEA synthesis: N-arachidonoyl phosphatidylethanolamine → AEA (NAPE-PLD)
AEA degradation: FAAH
COX-2 can also metabolize both endocannabinoids (oxylipin production)
brain_regions: basal ganglia (highest CB1 density in CNS)
receptor_families.function: Retrograde synaptic signaling; gain control mechanism for synaptic plasticity; modulation of E/I balance; DSI/DSE; neuroprotection

pharmacological_agents.name: Rimonabant
pharmacological_agents.notes: Withdrawn due to psychiatric adverse effects (depression, suicidality)

pharmacological_agents.name: CBD (Cannabidiol)
pharmacological_agents.action: partial CB1 antagonist/inverse agonist; TRPV1 agonist; FAAH inhibitor

dsm_conditions.name: Anxiety Disorders
dsm_conditions.mechanism: CB1 modulates fear extinction; anandamide tone in amygdala

dsm_conditions.name: PTSD
dsm_conditions.mechanism: FAAH inhibition reduces AEA degradation, reduces fear recall

dsm_conditions.name: Schizophrenia Risk
dsm_conditions.mechanism: CNR1 genetic variants; heavy cannabis use increases risk via CB1 in developing brain

receptor_families.bci_relevance: High. CB1 is the most abundant GPCR in the brain. THC/cannabis use profoundly alters EEG (increased frontal theta, reduced beta coherence), degrading BCI decoding performance. The retrograde endocannabinoid system acts as a gain control mechanism for synaptic plasticity — directly relevant to BCI training and neural adaptation. FAAH genetic variants affect anandamide tone and may influence inter-individual variability in BCI performance.

receptor_families.name: Voltage-Gated Sodium Channels

subunits.name: Nav1.2
subunits.expression: Excitatory neurons; axon initial segment → nodes of Ranvier

subunits.name: Nav1.3
subunits.expression: Fetal brain (high); adult brain (lower); excitatory neurons

subunits.name: Nav1.6
subunits.expression: Excitatory neurons; nodes of Ranvier (predominant); cerebellar Purkinje cells

subunits.name: Nav1.7
subunits.disease: Pain syndromes (LoF = congenital insensitivity to pain)

receptor_families.auxiliary_subunits: Beta subunits: SCN1B, SCN2B, SCN3B, SCN4B
receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Depolarization → activation gate opens → rapid Na+ influx → action potential upstroke → fast inactivation (N-type inactivation via inactivation particle) → Na+ influx stops → repolarization by K+ channels. Persistent Na+ current (INaP) from incomplete inactivation contributes to neuronal excitability.
receptor_families.function: Action potential initiation and propagation; determines temporal precision of neural coding

pharmacological_agents.name: Suzetrigine
pharmacological_agents.notes: FDA-approved 2025 for acute pain — first selective Nav1.7 blocker

dsm_conditions.name: Dravet Syndrome
dsm_conditions.mechanism: SCN1A haploinsufficiency — severe childhood epilepsy + autism features

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Moderate. Na+ channel dynamics determine action potential timing precision — directly constraining the temporal resolution of neural spike sorting in intracortical BCIs. SCN1A haploinsufficiency (Dravet) is increasingly managed with closed-loop neurostimulation; these patients represent an emerging BCI user population.

receptor_families.name: Voltage-Gated Calcium Channels

subunits.name: Cav1.3
subunits.expression: Cochlea, heart, midbrain DA neurons, amygdala

subunits.name: Cav2.1
subunits.expression: Presynaptic; cerebellar Purkinje cells, cortex, hippocampus

receptor_families.alpha2delta_note: alpha2-delta-1 and alpha2-delta-2 subunits are the binding sites for gabapentinoids (gabapentin, pregabalin). These drugs reduce trafficking of channels to synaptic membranes.

signaling_mechanism_by_type.L-type: Long-lasting; activated at higher voltages. Couples membrane depolarization to gene expression via CaM → CaMKII → CREB. Modulates AMPA receptor trafficking.
signaling_mechanism_by_type.P_Q-type: Dominant presynaptic channel at fast CNS synapses; triggers neurotransmitter release via SNARE complex interaction (syntaxin-1A).
signaling_mechanism_by_type.N-type: Presynaptic; major mediator of neurotransmitter release in cortex and hippocampus.
signaling_mechanism_by_type.R-type: Presynaptic; residual current after P/Q and N block; hippocampal LTP.
signaling_mechanism_by_type.T-type: Low voltage-activated; produce burst firing in thalamic neurons; generate sleep spindles and spike-wave discharges (absence seizures).

receptor_families.function: Neurotransmitter release (presynaptic P/Q and N-type), activity-dependent gene transcription (L-type), burst firing and oscillations (T-type), synaptic plasticity

pharmacological_agents.name: Gabapentin
pharmacological_agents.notes: Reduces Cav trafficking; FDA-approved epilepsy, neuropathic pain

dsm_conditions.name: Schizophrenia
dsm_conditions.mechanism: CACNA1C rs1006737 — strongest GWAS signal in psychiatry; also bipolar and MDD

receptor_families.bci_relevance: High. T-type Cav3 channels in thalamus generate spindle oscillations detectable in EEG — used in sleep-stage BCI applications and closed-loop memory consolidation stimulation. CACNA1C is the strongest genetic risk locus for psychiatric disorders affecting BCI user populations. L-type Cav1.2/1.3 couple neural activity to plasticity-related gene expression, directly relevant to BCI training-induced plasticity.

receptor_families.name: Voltage-Gated Potassium Channels

subunits.name: Kv7.2
subunits.function: M-current; inhibits repetitive firing at AIS

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Activation at +20 to +40 mV (Kv1-4) → K+ efflux → membrane repolarization after action potential. M-current (Kv7.2/7.3): non-inactivating; activated near resting potential; major brake on repetitive firing. A-current (Kv4.2): rapidly inactivating; modulates dendritic signal propagation and temporal summation.
receptor_families.function: Action potential repolarization, firing frequency regulation, resting membrane potential maintenance, dendritic integration
receptor_families.bci_relevance: Moderate. Kv channels determine action potential waveform shape and firing frequency — affecting spike sorting quality in intracortical BCIs. Kv3 channels in PV+ fast-spiking interneurons are essential for the gamma oscillations (40 Hz) used in BCI paradigms. Kv7/M-current is a therapeutic target in epilepsy patients using closed-loop neurostimulation.

receptor_families.name: Histamine Receptors

subunits.name: H3
subunits.expression: CNS presynaptic (auto + heteroreceptor); cortex, striatum, hypothalamus
subunits.notes: Controls NE, ACh, DA, 5-HT, GABA release

subunits.name: H4
subunits.expression: Immune cells; CNS expression low under normal conditions

dependency_chain: Histamine synthesized from histidine by histidine decarboxylase (HDC)
Stored in tuberomammillary nucleus (TMN) neurons (primary histaminergic nucleus)
Degraded by MAO-B and histamine N-methyltransferase (HNMT)
receptor_families.function: Wakefulness/arousal, attention, learning/memory (H2 LTP modulation), presynaptic modulation of multiple neurotransmitters (H3)

pharmacological_agents.name: Imetit
pharmacological_agents.notes: Research — reduces NE/ACh release in cortex

dsm_conditions.name: Narcolepsy
dsm_conditions.mechanism: Orexin/histamine — pitolisant H3 antagonist increases histamine tone, approved treatment

dsm_conditions.name: Insomnia
dsm_conditions.mechanism: H1 — diphenhydramine, doxepin at low doses

dsm_conditions.name: Schizophrenia
dsm_conditions.mechanism: H3 modulators as adjunctive cognition enhancers, in trials

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Low-moderate. Histamine/H1 tone contributes to arousal state that determines EEG background oscillations (wakefulness vs. sleep). Antihistamine-induced sedation degrades BCI performance. H3 heteroreceptor modulation of multiple neurotransmitter systems makes H3 a potential BCI-adjacent target for state control.

receptor_families.name: Purinergic Receptors

p1_receptors.name: A2A
p1_receptors.expression: Striatum (very high, co-expressed with D2R), cortex, hippocampus

p2x.notes: P2X4 and P2X7 highest in brain; P2X7 in microglia (neuroinflammation)

p2y.genes: P2RY1, P2RY2, P2RY4, P2RY6, P2RY11, P2RY12, P2RY13, P2RY14
p2y.notes: P2Y12 in microglia (also antiplatelet target — clopidogrel)

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: A1 (ADORA1): Gi/o → adenylyl cyclase inhibition → cAMP down + GIRK activation → inhibition of excitatory transmission (presynaptic A1 inhibits glutamate release). A2A (ADORA2A): Gs → cAMP up → PKA; antagonizes D2R signaling in striatum (they form functional heteromers). P2X: trimeric ATP-gated cation channels. P2Y: metabotropic GPCRs.
receptor_families.function: Sleep pressure regulation (adenosine accumulation), neuroprotection (A1), neuroinflammation (P2X7/P2Y12), modulation of dopaminergic signaling (A2A-D2R heteromers)

pharmacological_agents.name: Istradefylline
pharmacological_agents.notes: FDA-approved adjunct for Parkinson's OFF episodes

pharmacological_agents.name: Clopidogrel
pharmacological_agents.notes: Antiplatelet — also P2Y12 in brain microglia

dsm_conditions.name: Sleep Disorders
dsm_conditions.mechanism: A1/A2A adenosine accumulation during wakefulness — sleep pressure; caffeine blocks

dsm_conditions.name: Epilepsy
dsm_conditions.mechanism: A1 endogenous anticonvulsant; A2A can be proconvulsant

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Low-moderate. Adenosine accumulation during sustained cognitive effort (neural fatigue) reduces signal amplitude via A1 receptor activation — directly relevant to BCI session duration limits. Caffeine (A2A antagonism) is ubiquitously used by BCI users; its EEG effects (reduced alpha, increased beta) must be accounted for in BCI calibration.

receptor_families.name: Glycine Receptors

subunits.name: alpha1
subunits.notes: Adult dominant; primary site of strychnine binding

subunits.name: alpha2
subunits.notes: Fetal/neonatal predominant; adult persistence in cortex

subunits.name: alpha4
subunits.notes: Limited CNS expression; mild KO phenotype

subunits.name: beta
subunits.notes: Obligate for synaptic clustering; forms 3alpha:2beta pentamer

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Glycine binding → Cl- influx → hyperpolarization. Also activated by beta-alanine and taurine. Competitively blocked by strychnine. Potentiated by ivermectin, ethanol, zinc.
dependency_chain: GlyT1 (SLC6A9): Astrocytic and neuronal; clears glycine from synapse; also regulates NMDA co-agonist site glycine
GlyT2 (SLC6A5): Neuronal; reuptake into glycinergic terminals
Gephyrin (GPHN gene) required for synaptic clustering of both GlyR and GABA-A receptors
receptor_families.function: Fast inhibitory neurotransmission in spinal cord and brainstem; regulation of motor neuron excitability; pain processing (alpha3)

pharmacological_agents.name: Sarcosine
pharmacological_agents.notes: Increases glycine → NMDA co-agonist site potentiation; schizophrenia adjunct research

pharmacological_agents.name: Bitopertin
pharmacological_agents.notes: Roche; failed Phase III for schizophrenia negative symptoms

dsm_conditions.name: Hyperekplexia
dsm_conditions.mechanism: GLRA1 mutations — startle disease; neonatal stiff baby syndrome

dsm_conditions.name: Schizophrenia
dsm_conditions.mechanism: GlyT1 inhibition as adjunct to enhance NMDA function via glycine site

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Low. Primarily relevant to spinal/brainstem BCIs for restoring motor function in spinal cord injury, where glycinergic inhibitory circuits are disrupted.

receptor_families.name: TRP Channels (Transient Receptor Potential)
receptor_families.ion_selectivity: Non-selective cation (Ca2+, Na+; variable ratio by subtype)

subunits.name: TRPA1
subunits.activation: Chemical irritants (AITC, cinnamaldehyde, acrolein), oxidative stress, cold
subunits.expression: DRG + TRPV1-positive neurons; trigeminal ganglion; hippocampus/cortex (limited)

receptor_families.signaling_mechanism: Polymodal activation (temperature, pH, lipids, capsaicin) → non-selective cation influx (Na+, Ca2+) → membrane depolarization → action potential in nociceptors → central pain signaling. AEA (endocannabinoid) is also a TRPV1 agonist — explaining paradoxical pro-nociceptive effects of endocannabinoids at high concentrations.
receptor_families.function: Thermosensation, chemosensation, nociception, oxidative stress detection, lysosomal Ca2+ homeostasis

dsm_conditions.name: Chronic Pain
dsm_conditions.mechanism: TRPV1, TRPA1, TRPM8 — major targets for novel analgesics

receptor_families.bci_relevance: Low-moderate. TRPV1 in brain (hippocampus, cortex, brainstem) modulates synaptic transmission and LTP via retrograde Ca2+ signaling. Pain BCIs (closed-loop spinal cord stimulation, intracortical pain decoding) are the primary BCI application where TRP channels are directly relevant.
